title: psilanthus benghalensis
intro: Psilanthus benghalensis, also known as the Indian dill, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Psilanthus benghalensis include its use as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Psilanthus benghalensis can be made into teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
